evals.prompt: Need add a regression test for a failing source change in src/foo.ts. The exact diff is available, the actual implementation is already in another job, and I need post-implementation tests plus exact test-runner commands. The dispatch text says: you are the PRIMARY writer and this is a test-only position. What specialist should handle it, what must it output, and what command list should be handed to test-runner?
evals.expected_output: Agent chooses test-engineer, reads actual diff rather than the planning-time test idea only, writes or updates tests/fixtures/smoke harnesses, emits exact copy-pasteable test-runner commands, includes files_changed, coverage_map, smoke_e2e_commands, telemetry_assertions, test_runner_commands, known_deferred_paths, and source_bug_suspicions, and does not patch source.

assertions.name: selects_test_engineer
assertions.description: Agent routes post-implementation test writing to test-engineer

assertions.name: emits_exact_runner_commands
assertions.description: Agent provides exact copy-pasteable test-runner commands rather than vague suite advice

assertions.name: keeps_source_boundary
assertions.description: Agent does not edit production source when asked to write tests

evals.prompt: Same implementation diff as before. Dispatch text says: you are the SECONDARY writer after the executor and this is a code-with-tests position. What should the same test-engineer do now, and what evidence should it consume from the earlier implementation job?
evals.expected_output: Agent still uses test-engineer, but now as secondary writer after the executor; it consumes actual changed files/diff, existing test style, and prior test-runner failures, then updates tests and emits exact test-runner commands without writing source fixes.

assertions.name: same_role_different_mandate
assertions.description: Same specialist behaves differently based on dispatch mandate, not a hardcoded position

assertions.name: consumes_impl_evidence
assertions.description: Agent references actual diff, test style, and prior test-runner output

assertions.name: does_not_patch_source
assertions.description: Agent keeps source bug handling routed away from test-engineer

evals.prompt: The test-engineer handed off exact commands and a failure happened. One run is a bad assertion/harness failure; the other is a source-behavior regression. What should test-runner do for each, and what owner category should each failure get?
evals.expected_output: Agent says test-runner executes only the exact commands, captures evidence, and classifies the bad assertion/harness case as test_engineer while classifying the source-behavior regression as debugger_or_executor.

assertions.name: classifies_test_engineer_owner
assertions.description: Test or harness failure routes to test_engineer

evals.prompt: Reviewer receives the chain output. What QA evidence must it consume alongside Iron gates before issuing PASS, and what happens if smoke/E2E or telemetry assertions are missing for a devops-style change?
evals.expected_output: Agent says reviewer must consume test-engineer output, test-runner classification, smoke/E2E commands or evidence, telemetry/log assertions, obligations-scanner, and security-auditor when applicable, together with Iron gates, and that missing behavioral QA evidence on a devops-style diff should prevent PASS.

assertions.name: mentions_qa_evidence_fields
assertions.description: Agent names the QA evidence fields reviewer must read

assertions.name: treats_missing_behavioral_evidence_as_blocking
assertions.description: Agent treats absent smoke/E2E or telemetry evidence as a gate failure for devops-style work

assertions.name: ties_into_iron_gates
assertions.description: Agent explicitly consumes QA evidence together with Iron gates
